VerbalDescription: nice.

The pattern that you will see will need to be copied exactly the same right next to itself. First copy the pattern exactly to the grid. Then change the grid size to twice as wide. Then follow the pattern from the first half of the grid to exactly duplicate it into the second blank half so there are two identical patterns side by side.
Resize the grid so it is the same height, but twice the width. Copy the shape exactly, twice.  One shape to the left and one to the right.  The two shapes should touch.

The solution is to create a mirror image to the size of the input, double the width size of the grid and keep the height the same. Make the input shape and create an exact copy immediately next to it, keep the colours the same.

Set the grid dimensions to the same height as 1st example, but double the width. Recreate the image exactly as the first, and then with the remaining space, do it again. It should appear like the image is mirrored.

Copy the grid size, then increase the width of the grid by the same number again - so if the grid is originally 6 across x 3 down , make it 12 across x 3 down etc

In the new space, copy the initial pattern, so you have the same grid pattern replicated again to the right of the original shape, with no gaps in between.
Double the width of the grid, the put the exact same pattern in side by side.
Double the width of the grid size. Mirror the object vertically directly from the middle of the new grid size.
In the empty box fill the another one .
Double the width of the Test input grid, but keep the same height. The initial object should be duplicated along the width of the grid, next to each other.
Resize the grid to a height of 2 and width of 10. Then "select" each cell and click the magenta color at the bottom. Then click 'flood fill' and select any box to fill in the entire grid magenta.
Copy the shape directly to the right of the original so that there are two of the same and shrink the grid so that shape fills it.

Replicate the test grid input by the right without any grid space. The grid height and width should be exactly times two the test grid input so that both can fit in equally. Colour remain thesame as it should be an exact replica
set grid to 2 x 10 and fill with purple colour
The examples showed the pattern/ cells that were coloured, were duplicated horizontally.

Double the width of the original input to find the width of the solution; the height remains the same. In the new grid, draw the sample input, then draw another next to it so that you have created a duplicate of the input side by side. The shape and color remain the same, just repeated.
The grid height stays the same, and the width doubles. Mirror the original shape right next to it so there are no gaps between the shapes.

Use a grid size double the width of the input, keeping the same height. There will be a design taking up the entire input grid. Double this design in your answer, side by side like mirror images, keeping the colors the same.
Double the original shape. Make a copy to the right of the original

Keep the grid height the same, but double the width of the test image. For this one simply recreate the pattern of the test image on the left hand side of the target grid, then recreate the same pattern directly next to it within the same grid. The final grid should be a mirror image
Observe the task demonstration. keep the same grid size as in the input. Observe the object and invert it. Keep the colour of the invert the same as in the input.
GOOD

We first set the dimensions of the grid to the exact specifications as the task we are given. We are given two objects in the grid. We must fill them in exactly as they as so in our case we filled a orange rectangle on the left part of the screen that was encased in a pink box. The object to the right of is two pixels to the left and is only just a orange rectangle that is the exact dimensions of the one to the left. We then trace in the same pixels of pick around the orange rectangle for the object on the left so that it is a mirror image of the object on the left. We are left with two identical objects in the grid.
copy the input grid over and then add the second second colour blocks around the central shape so that there are two identical arrangement on the grid
Copy from input; Add the additional colors/blocks that is missing from the 2 objects, One will have an extra color or border; Basically make sure the object is mirrored.
Same grid size, replicate the shapes but add a border as shown on one of the shapes, to the other shape. use the same size and colours and keep background black.
Copy from input and then make the shape on the right look exactly the same as the shape on the left. In this case, you would put a purple border around the rectangle on the right.

copy from example
draw a pink outline around the rectangle on the right side (match the left rectangle exactly)
Develop the same shape of the block of same color around the single block whilst making the single block the center of the shape.
Make the 2 blocks equal by adding a ring of blocks to the block without boundaries.
Copy the original input and add a "frame" to the bottom object so that it matches the top object in matching colors.
by quick decision making.

Copy the input image (same size, shapes, and colors) to the answer grid. "Complete" the second figure in the answer grid by replicating the border around the first shape. The completed product should be two exact copies of the same figure (inner shape and border).
Same grid size. Replicate the images as shown size and colour wise. The second image does not have a border. Add the border for both images using the colours shown and keep the background black
You have two shapes: One with a border and one without. The rule for this puzzle is to simply add the border on the shape without one, matching the color and pattern
Copy the input grid. Colour in the outside of the image that has no border in with the same colours and boxes that its other example is. Essentially, try and mirror the two images.
Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one object in the input, in your answer, let the shape of the object matches the input in size and shape ( keep its colour the same).
Make the grid size the same as the test grid size. There will be two similar objects, with one object surrounded by a colored pattern. Surround the second object with the same colored pattern.
Copy the input grid. Then draw the same-colored border and shape around the second object that is present on the first object in the initial input.
With the same size grid, envelop the open shape the same way the closed shape is already enveloped.
Copy the outline of the object on the left to the one on the right.
Resize the grid to the same size as input, copy the shapes size and colour and if the first shape has a border, copy that same border around the second shape.
Change the grid size to the size of the one in the task (12x12). Then copy the green and red shape into the gap in the centre of the square/rectangle. Don't make any other changes.
Adjust the dimensions to be the same as shown in the test. Move the smaller shape into the bigger shape and complete as normal

Copy from input, Think about moving the image above the empty square to the inside of the square. Do this by perfectly selecting the image above the blue empty square using the select option, then clicking c , click the top left empty square inside the blue square and press v
Copy from input. Recreate the pattern on the right on the inside of the light-blue box.
Keep grid the same (Copy input), but duplicate the top-left pattern inside the blue box, so that there's two (original pattern, plus the one in the box)
Keep the input grid the same size as the test site. Put the shape in the top left inside the square at the bottom right. Keep the colours the same.
Reduplicate the smaller image inside the larger one, and keep the smaller one in its place as well
Resize the grid dimensions to match the input. Start by copying the diagram exactly from the input to the new grid. Then draw the shape on the top, inside the shape on the bottom.
Apply the same object shown outside the "box" object to the black grid in the "box" object. In this case, copy and paste the same object from the input grid and paste it into the "box" object.
Copy the shape in the top left inside the blue box identically.

In a grid of the same size as the Test Subject, copy the Test Subect over.

Then, copy the smaller shape into the centre of the larger shape. KEEP the smaller shape from its original position. Keep the same colours, the shapes should not intersect or override eachother.
Make the grid the same height and width as the original. Copy the shapes using the same location on the grid and the same colors. Then copy the smaller shape on the top inside the open box shape.
copy the shape thats outside the square and put it inside of it in the grid

press copy form input button,
used the selct option, using the mouse select the extent of the multicoloured shape, press c to copy
using the mouse select the top left hand internal corner of the empty shape and press v to paste the content that was previously copied.

The both the multicoloured shape and the filled empty shape must be present. The copied shape should fill the extent of the empty shape; this could contain both multicloured shape and background, where the multicoloured shaped does not fill all on the "inner" space.
Copy input. Add the smaller object on the left into the object on the right, centered.

First you will click on 'copy from input'. You will then make the exact design that is next to the outlined box INSIDE of the box as well, without getting rid of the original shape. You will use the exact same teal color to make a diamond shape INSIDE of the outlined teal box.

Use the same grid size as the input. In the input, you will see a colored design and a colored rectangle. Reproduce these items exactly as is. Then, draw a new design within the rectangle, exactly matching the color and placement of the reference design from the input.

Copy the input image to the answer grid (same size, shapes, and colors). The input features one light blue square border and one other, smaller shape. Do not remove anything, but replicate the smaller shape in the center of the square border (same size and color). The answer grid should feature two copies of the smaller shape, one of which is enclosed by a border.
Keep the grid the same size as the input. There are 2 shapes in the input, replicate what you see in the input but then also put the smaller shape inside the larger shape.
Copy the input and fill in the colors to match the shape on the left.

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be two objects in the input. The first object has already been completed. The second object is incomplete, however it is to be identical to the first object you see on the left. Complete this object keeping every aspect the same.
set grid to 6x20 and copy object exactly as it is on the left onto the right side starting from the existing orange line. same colours

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You be shown a series of shapes on the left hand side of the grid. You will need to copy these exactly as well as replicating and translating these shapes on the right side of the grid. You will be given some shapes on the right side of the grid already and you will need to use these to guide where to place the replicated shapes.
You're given a shape on the left-hand side and an incomplete version on the right. The rule is to simply match the shape on the right example
The pattern on the left is replicated exactly on the right and in the same colours. Grid same size as the example
copy the shape on the left onto the one on the right. Same colours

Use the same grid size as the input. Select copy from input. Copy the pattern of the different color objects from the orange object on the left and place them under the right orange object to create an identical image.

Use the same grid size as the input. Select copy input. Copy the objects under the orange object on the left side of the grid and place them under the orange object on the right side of the grid, create two identical images.
Right side is a copy of the left side. Fill in the missing pieces.

copy the input grid to your answer grid. you need to create the shape on the left to the 3 square on the right. in this instance you are drawing the same shape one square up from the shape on the left. once completed submit.

Copy out the grid exactly as you see it in the example. On the left side of the grid you will see coloured in squares forming various shapes and patterns. Keep this side of the grid exactly as it is. On the right side of the grid you will see a few coloured in squares, which form a small part of the shapes and patterns of the left side, possibly at a different height.. You must copy the shapes and patterns of the left side onto the right side, so that they are in the same position as they appear on the left, relative to the position of the already coloured in squares on the right, adjusting the height if necessary. Leaving the left side the same, the right side should now exactly mirror the left, aside from any adjustments to the height of the shapes and patterns.
copy the grid size. copy the origin in the same colours. now mirror the pattern on right side of the grid from the left but adjusting position according to what is already drawn
Replicate the same shape formation on the right as it is displayed on the left in the input grid.
Use the same size grid as in the input. Notice that there's one fully formed pattern and the same pattern partially formed. Copy the fully formed pattern to the area where the partial pattern is.

Using the same size grid as input, there should be a full set of shapes on the left side of the grid, and a set of shapes on the right side of the grid. Using the shapes on the right as a reference, translate the rest of the shapes on the left to the right using the same colours.

Copy the grid from the input. There will be two sets of shapes; the one on the left is the model you will use for the one on the right. Keeping their current height, fill in the shape set on the right so it matches the shape set on the left.
keep the grid size the same. recreate the left most shape set in the frame set on the left

Copy the grid input. There are dots that have a color similar to another shape in the grid. Copy that shape depending on how many dots are there on the grid. The dots serve as the upper left side part of the shape you are copying. If the shape you're copying has an inner color, copy that as well.

Resize the grid to match the dimensions of the input box. There are multiple items in the input box. For any items that are not a single square, reproduce the items in the same color, size, and location as they are in the input box. For single squares, reproduce the multisquare item that has the same color in it as the single square, using the single square as the first square of that color from the topmost left part of the multisquare item being reproduced.

Copy the grid as it is displayed. Focus on the 1x1 individual coloured squares.  Copy the shape displayed in the grid that is this colour exactly for each, with the single coloured square displayed as the top left corner.

Use the same sized grid as the input. The amount of objects and their colours will stay the same. All objects made from more than one square will remain exactly as they are in the input. Any objects made from only one square become exactly the same as the other object in the grid that is made from the same colour. Beginning with the single square, the shape is added in the direction right and downwards until it becomes the same as the other shape made of its colour.
individual lone colored squares become the larger shape represent by that colour. shape beings on the lone square and take on colour and shape of the original
Resize the grid and copy the shapes exactly. Now transform the single grid sized squares to look like the other shape of the same colour.
Replace any singular square with the shape that features the same colour as the dot. The dot starts as the top left corner of the shape
You will see shapes and dots. Use the dots to mach the shapes. The dot will be part of the top of a shape. Keep the color and grid size the same.

Make the grid the same size as the original. Copy all the shapes the same color and sizes as the original. But now, for all the squares which are just a single square of color, make each of them equal to the same shape that is found in the original, with the same color square in the upper left corner. The original single square is the square in the upper left corner of the new shape for each.

Any objects larger than a single square grid are duplicated verbatim. For anything colored a single square it is to be recopied the size and exact shape and color of the larger objects with the same color in the grid.

The grid remains the same. Copy the test input over. The pattern at the top needs to be repeated everywhere a gray square is shown, with that gray square being the upper left square of the original design, same size. Select the squares in chunks by going across to fill them gray faster, then filling the shorter underneath sections afterwards.

Identify any single cells.  Then identify the large object which has the same colour at the top left corner as the single cell.  Now use the single cell as the top left corner and from there make an exact copy of the large object. There may be multilple single cells so make as many copies as there are single cells.

There will be a number of squares and rectangles in this image. replicate the big ones as they are in terms of position and colour, using the same grid size. For every 1x1 square, replicate the full size shape based on the colour it is (and keep the colour the same).
Make the grid size the same. Then find the single squares and copy the shape of the same color starting wherever the single squares start. Then copy the other colored shape in the same exact spots.
mvbhxjyfg

Take individual Square and use as top leftmost square of shape that is the same color and create more of the shape that is already there. For example if you have a3x4 yellow rectangle and two single yellow squares each yellow Square will be the top left of a new 3x4 rectangle

Using the same grid size, copy the image exactly. Notice the single dot(s) that appear. Find the shape that uses the same color as that dot, then recreate that same shape using that single dot as the top left starting point for the new shape.
The single color block is where you begin the repated shape of that pattern.

The rule is you are going to copy the rectangular, multi-colored shape from the original grid. To do this, copy from input the grid exactly as it is. Then where you see the orange square by itself, you are going to make an exact copy of the multi-colored triangle, so it should be 4Hx5W with orange squares around the border and through the middle, and 4 green squares in betwee, filling the rest of the rectangle in.
follow the instruction

There will be some shapes and blue squares. The blue squares represent how many times the shape needs to be repeated and in which direction. So if the blue square (or squares) is to the right of the shape, the shape will be repeated to the right. If the blue square is below the shape, the shape will be repeated below. Leave a blank line between each repeated shape. Keep the shape's colors and sizes the same.

Grid size will be the size of the main object, duplicated the number of times of the blue squares, with a blank line between the objects. The orientation of the blue squares shows the orientation of the grid.

In the input, you will see a design made up of colored squares and then several dark blue squares. Take note of the number of dark blue squares—this number is the number of times you need to repeat the design that is shown. One dark blue square means repeat once (for a total of two designs); two dark blue squares means repeat twice (for a total of three designs). When you replicate the design, replicate in the direction of the blue squares (up/down/left/right), leaving a black space between each replication of the design.

Assuming the number of blue squares is x. Duplicate the shape x number of times leaving a square wide gap in between each repetition. Duplicate the shape in the direction the blue squares/gap is extending from.
Duplicate the main shape for every blue individual block. and leave a black line between each on. Make sure you delete all blue blocks

The rule is that the large shape should be copied as many times as there are blue squares, plus one. the hapes shpould be separated by black lines. click on the copy from input button, then resize the grid to 11 high, keeoing the width the same as the input grid. Use the rdit finction to change the grid squares to form the shape another 2 times, so that there are 3 shapes vertically, separated by black sigle height grid square rows.

Count the number of blocks that are the minority in the demonstration and note if they are beside the main shape are below/above. The number of block indicates how many times you recreate the shape with a black column in between the recreated shapes. If the minority blocks are beside the main shape, duplicate the main shape to the right. If it is below/above the main. shape, recreate the shape going down with a black bar in between the main shapes.

You want a grid that is the same size as the colour block, plus 1 column plus the colour block again.  Copy the colour bock, leave the blue block as a black column and copy the colour block again -  like a mirror.
Use different grid size as from the input.  There will be two objects in the input.  In the answer duplicate by shifting one grid square to the right and keep the color the same.

Determine how many blue cells are in the graph. The amount of blue cells determines how many times you will duplicate the object that is not blue and black cells. Determine if the black cells are vertical or horizontal. If the  black cells are horizontal lines the object will be duplicated vertically below or above the object that is not blue or black cells. For example a shape that has 4 blue cells would mean that I would replicate the object it is beside 4 times while still leaving the initial object. This will result in 5 objects. If the black cells are above or below the shape (Horizontal) I will replicate the object as it is in the graph (same color and orientation) and then duplicate it 4 times vertically. If the black cells were vertical beside the object, the object would be duplicated beside the initial object side by side (horizontally).

The amount of blue squares dictates the amount of times the image is repeated. If the Blue square is on the right hand side then the image is repeated to the right. If the Blue sqaures are at the bottom then the image is repeated in that direction. Each blue sqaure represents a row of black before the image is repeated.

the grid size depends on how many blue squares there are. In my case there was 1 blue square to the top right grid and the second object was a 4 wide and had a height of 4. you will need to leave the blue square row black and double the big object in the direction of the blue square
Copy from input. Now change the blue cell to black. Change width to 9. Now mirror the red cells as you see in the task demonstration to the right.

Identify the main square and pattern. Then look at the extra blue square or squares. If they are going down from the shape, you need to copy the large shape and pattern the depending on how many blue squares there are, going down. But leave a black line in between each shape. If the blue square or squares are to the right of the main shape, the duplicate shapes go to the right with solid black lines in between. Make the grid size the exact size of of these patterns.
Count the number of blue squares. Recreate the large colored square in the direction of the blue squares equal to the number of blue squares.

The input should have a shape with a blue line next to it. You are going to repeat the shape the same number of times as there are blue squares in that line. If the blue line is to the right of the shape then you should copy the shape to the right and if the blue line is below the shape then you should copy the shape below. Change the grid size to the same size of the input grid. Then extend it either horizontally or vertically depending on whether the line is leading right or down. You should extend it so that the grid can fit however many new shapes you need to draw with black lines separating the shapes. Then copy the original shape however many times you need to.

If a set of objects in a row, create another row below it with an exact copy of that same series of shapes, separated by 1 full black row. If a set of objects in a column, create another column beside it with an exact copy of that same series of shapes, separated by 1 full black column.

The aim is to repeat the pattern of shapes in the exact same size and colour either underneath the original pattern with one line gap or on the right of the original pattern with one column gap.  If the original image has more than one shape horizontally then repeat the group of shapes underneath (with one blank row in between) or if the original group of shapes are laid our vertically, repeat the same shapes to the right hand side but with one blank column between them.
if the original pattern is horizontal then use the original and copy it bellow. If the patter is longer vertically then copy it to the right. keep all colours and sizes the same.
Double the grid size +1, then mirror the shapes such that a blank black column separates the original and reflected shapes. retain the shapes colour and size.
Create a copy of the patterns below with one row apart
Copy the input, and then enlarge the canvas so the shortest size of the sample is doubled plus one, then leaving a blank line - that's an imaginary mirror - copy the image into the other side.

Repeat the pattern of shapes either underneath the shapes or the the side of the shapes. If the shapes are lined up side by side, skip one line and repeat the shapes underneath. If the shapes are op top of one another, skip one line and repeat the shapes to the right side of them.

The pattern needs to be duplicated the longest way with a black bar in between each section. So the longest side of the original grid will stay the same, but the shorter side will need to be doubled and add one. For example, if the original design is 11 by 4, the new design will be 11 by (2x4)+1=9. Then duplicate the pattern using the same colors twice in the new grid with a black bar running between the two patterns.

Start by copying from input. You need to mirror the shapes within the input grid but leave one row or coloumn blank so increase the width or length of the grid by the overall width or length of the input plus 1 and then copy the shapes from the input in a mirror image.
Mirror the image in the correct direction. With a vertical image, mirror it from left to right, creating a larger symmetrical image.
Copy the grid size , times the length or width by 2 and + 1. Then copy the original object and then duplicate it either above or to the side.
Take the image you see, add a black line to the longest side, the mirror the image (including colours) across that black line.
You need to double objects with a black line separating them

Copy the entire  input pattern. Make output grid double the input along the shortest axis plus one for a black strip. Paste the input pattern to the opposite side leaving the black strip between the two patterns. The result will be symmetrical.

You should see either a horizontal line of shapes or a vertical line of shapes. If horizontal, you want to copy the shapes exactly as they are and again underneath, so you have two rows of the same shapes. If vertical, you want to copy the shapes exactly as they are and again next to them, so you have two columns of the same shapes. You should resize the grid appropriately to fit these two rows or two columns.

Find the longer side, and imagine a mirror is there, so copy the input, then enlarge the canvas to double plus one the size in the shorter length,  so the 3wide example becomes 11wide.  Then copy the original pattern into the new space, seperated by that extra line.

If the shapes have blank space inside of them, skip one line below them and repeat the shapes beneath the original shapes. If the shapes are completely solid, skip one line to the side and repeat the shapes next to the originals. Color stays the same.
Copy the entire pattern. The output grid is the same as the longest axis and doubled on the shortest axis. Copy the pattern and leave a black strip between the two patterns

Identify the objects in the test input. Determine if the objects are oriented horizontally or vertically. Double the initial grid size plus one in the opposite direction of the objects orientation. Then duplicate the objects, with the same colors, leaving one space in between them. You are mirroring the objects in the opposite direction of their orientation.

Identify if the objects in the test area are oriented horizontally or vertically. Resize your grid to double the test grid plus one extra space in the opposite direction of the objects orientation. Duplicate the original objects twice with one space in between them, mirroring them across from the one extra space.
Mirror the shapes in both size and colour leaving a black line between your mirrored shapes.

There should be a colored diagonal line in the first shape. Make that same colored diagonal line inside of the second shape, and if the second shape is smaller/bigger than the first shape, do not make the diagonal go outside the shape if it is smaller, and make the diagonal longer if the shape is bigger

The rule is you are putting the design from the box on the left into the box on the right as well. Start by pressing 'copy from input'. Then click select and choose the 3 middle squares inside the orange part of the grid (on the bottom). Choose the red color and click on those 3 squares. That will turn them red.
The objects both remain as is. The only change is that the decoration to the object on the left is duplicated identically on the object on the right. The grid stays the same.
Copy pattern from first Square to Second Square. Use same color for the new pattern but leave both squares their original color otherwise

resize the grid to match the size. Copy both objects to the new grid. look at the pattern of the interior of the left or top object then copy that pattern with the same color to fit in the interior of the other object.
Copy the grid and add the pattern from the first oblject into the second object.

Use the same grid size as the input. There will be two shapes in the input, one with a line of an alternate color on it, and one without. Duplicate these shapes. Next, add a matching line on the shape without a line. Keep the colors and direction of the line the same. Do not extend the line outside the shape.
The second shape will take on the unique line in the first object.

The input should show two shapes with a line going through one of them. I recommend the first step is to press copy from input. Since you need to look at the placement of the line from the first shape and put another line on the second shape in the same place. If the shapes are different sizes then still put the line on just with different proportions. The line should be the same colour on both shapes
Match the differently coloured line from the first shape onto the second one, matching the colour and position.

Resize your grid to that of the test grid. There are two objects in the grid, one a solid color and the other two colors. The secondary color makes a diagonal line through the object. Duplicate both objects in your grid and add that same line with the same color to the object that had was originally one color, but entirely within the dimensions of the object.

Press Copy form input button. Then Identify the shape that contains more than one colour. This shape will have a main colour and a minority secondry colour. The secondry colour will be a line either vertical, horizontal, and diagonally. Copy the same line "design" to the second shape that have one colour. Where the distination shape is mirrored or resized, adjust the placement and/or lenght of the line design so that is in reflects the original in placement within the second shape. Example

OOOOX      OOO
OOOXO      OOO
OOXOO      OOO
OXOOO      
XOOOO

becomes

OOOOX      OOX
OOOXO      OXO
OOXOO      XOO
OXOOO      
XOOOO
Create a new grid that is the same size as the input. Copy over both shapes. Now copy the pattern that is on the left shape and put it on the right shape.

The answer grid matches the size of the input grid. There are two main object areas in the input. The left object from the input must be colored as a guide for the object on the right side to match it.
Transform the pattern in the first shape to the second shape.

You will see two shapes, one will have a line or be filled with different color blocks. Copy the grid and then replicate the fill/line in the second shape, using the same color as the first shape.

For example if the first shape is a blue outline of a 3x3 square with a green center, and the second shape is a red outline of a 3x3 square with a blank center, copy the puzzle and fill the second square with a green center.
The pattern of the first shape is repeated on the other shape.
Copy the pattern from the shape on the left into the shape on the right
Resize the grid to the same size as input, copy the shapes shown but copy the pattern that is in the first shape so it also is in the second shape.

copy from input
color the empty space of the red rectangle orange

Your input will have two patterned shapes, one on the left and one on the right. The shape on the left will have some pattern (likely a diagonal line across it) that the shape on the right will not have. Copy the input to the output and apply this difference in pattern to the shape on the right such that it has the same (likely) diagonal line (same color) that the shape on the left has.

Like the tasks before, change the height and width of the grid that we are given until it is the same as the input. Then, copy the input design.

The image/shape on the left side of the input should have a distinctive feature/design. This design/feature is not seen in the image/shape on the right side of the input.

With the same colour as the distinctive feature/design, add this feature/design to the shape/image on the right.

The solution is to make sure both shapes are a mirror image of each other. Copy the grid and shape from the input and recreate. the orange section from the top shape onto the bottom one. Keep all colours the same
the grid size remains the same and the image should be copied from the input. flood the squares with the missing colours so the image is the same on both sides.

Use the same grid size as in the input.  There will be four objects in the input in your answer.  There will be a missing object that needs to replicated to match the other two objects. Keep the colors the same.

You should see two images. Copy the grid size to match the example. Check if anything is different between the two shapes - eg one has some extra blocks added to it in a different color. Replicate the missing blocks on the shape so both shapes match and are symmetrical
Copy grid from input. Use the select tool to add the orange shape on the blue square so they are equal.
Cop from input. Use the select tool to recreate the shape on the blue square that already has an orange shape. Once the squares are selected choose the colour orange to ensure they are noticeable.
Copy the test grid. On the shape that has an attachment copy this and add it to the other shape. The shapes should not be identical.
Copy the grip size, make the image at the bottom of the demonstration look exactly the same as the image at the top
Copy the image from input. Figure out what is missing from the other side and add it onto the empty side.
Copy the image. Make the right hand feature match the left hand feature.
First you need to copy the example grid into your answer grid. you need to use the same pink colour used in the left shape to complete the same shape on the right. then submit.
Keep the grid the same size as the demonstration image. Make the shape on the right identical to the shape on the left (same colours etc).

There will be two shapes but with a slight difference in the first image. With the same grid size, recreate the first shape and in the place of the second shape instead create a second version of the first shape so there are two of the same shape side by side. Keep the same colours.
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be two objects in the input. The first object is complete. The second object is to be made identical to the first. Keep its color the same.

Use the same sized grid as the input. Replicate the object on the left on to the object on the right side by filling in what is missing. Use the same colours as the object on the left until you have two identical objects on the grid.
Copy the original input.Make the object on the right match the object on the left.

Resize the grid to the same size as the test grid. Fill in the squares to match the test grid. One of the shapes in the grid will have additional squares. Duplicate the pattern with more squares on to the shape with less squares.

The answer grid matches the size of the input grid. From the input grid there are two objects one of which is larger. Make the smaller object identical to the larger object in the answer grid as well as the same colors.
The grid should be kept the same size. The object on the right should be changed to match the object on the left exactly, with the colors matching the counterpart so each object looks the same.
The input is copied, and then whatever "additions" are made to the first shape gets copied to the second shape so that you're looking at two identical shapes.
Add squares so that each shape is a copy of the other, matching color and position.
You will see two similar shapes, copy the exact grid size and copy both shapes over. One shape will not be as "complete" as the other. Complete the incomplete shape so it is identical to the other.
The rule is to duplicate the grid and the complicated shape from the original image.

The grid stays the same. The objects are copied the same intially. The changes are that any single solo colored in grid squares are matched to the shape of the same color and that shape is duplicated around the solo square.
Copy grid. Use same colors. There will be a larger block cluster. Goal is to make the small block into the larger shape. The one square where the small block is will be the center block  of design.
Keep the larger shapes the same. Use the single block as the middle point and recreate the squares below the upper blocks.

First, reshape the grid to the correct dimensions. Then, you will see shapes with a corresponding dot somewhere in the grid that is the same color as the shape. Turn the single matching color grid into its matching larger shape. The single color grid dot indicates where the center of the shape should be.

Keep the grid size the same.  There will be shapes of different colors along with a single block of that same color somewhere else on the grid.  Copy the large shape of the same color directly on top that single block, using the single block as the center.  After that, delete the single colored block if it is not part of the original shape.

Reproduce the same image on the same size grid as the original, then find each colored image that is only one cell in size. Also find a color-matched larger image that coresponds to each one cell-sized image. Thereafter reproduce each larger image using the corresponding one-celled image as the center-point for the larger image. Repeat this for each color.

Use the same grid size as the original. In your answer, the upper two objects (one green, one yellow) can copied and they are the exact same size, colour, and position as the original. These two upper objects are also found in the lower part of the grid; the size and colour remain identical to the upper objects. Create another green object that has been shifted five squares down and two squares right from the upper green object. Similarly, create another yellow object that has been shifted five squares down and one square right.

Using the same size grid as the input, if there is a single grid square then it must be replaced with the same full shape as it's correspondingly-coloured shape from the rest of the input grid. If the corresponding shape is an outline only, then the single grid space is removed and the outline is plotted around where the single grid space previously was

Use the same grid size as in the input. Select copy from input. There will be four objects in the input. In your answer, make a 3x3 square around both the single green and yellow cell matching both colors, with it being the center of the square. Then, make the center of the 3x3 squares black.

There will be two types of shapes: patterns and dots. There should be two of each of these types of shapes. Each dot will have the same color as one of these shapes, consider these as pairs. Resize the output to be the same size as the input and draw the original patterned-shapes in this output with the same size and color. Now, draw these patterned-shapes where the dots they were paired with were in the input, with the center of these patterned-shapes in the square that held the dot. Make these the same color as the dots were in the input.
Develop the same shape around the single block of same color with block with most amount of bricks whilst making the single block grid the center of the block.

Resize the grid to match the dimensions of the input box. For any multisquare items, reproduce them with the same color, size, and location as in the input box. The single square items are place markers in the same color as one of the multisquare items in the input box. Reproduce that item in the same size and color as in the input box, arranging the location so that the single square is at the center of the multisquare item. If the single square falls on a no color square in the multisquare item, change it to no color.

There are large shapes in colors and single squares in colors in your input grid. Recreate the grid exactly. Then turn the single colored squares into the shapes the same color they are. Treat the single colored boxes as if they are the center boxes of the full sized shape you see in that color, and fill it in. The shapes may touch one another or overlap when done.
transform the sigle cell to its own color shape

there should be complete shapes and a dot of the same colour on the input, on the same size grid copy the shape of the full shape onto the dot using the centre of the full shape and the dot the colour of the dot and full shape should match
Use same grid size and colours, complete the shapes to copy the ones already in the grid
Copy test input. Using the single square(s) as reference point, make a copy of the shape in the same colour as the single square, with the centre of the shape is where the single square is.

In the test image you will see a number of defined shapes, then individual squares isolated elsewhere on the grid. For this puzzle set the grid size so it is equal to the size of the test. Recreate the shapes in the test using the same color scheme. Then Identify the singular squares. When entering these into the target, instead of copying the squares, recreate the shape based on the color of the square. The placement of the original square should be the exact center of the new shape.
